hero.title: From Theory to Installation:
hero.subtitle: A Complete Solar Education
hero.description: This is not just a course; it's a career path. Our "blended learning" model combines flexible online modules with essential, hands-on workshop training. You will learn the 'why' and the 'how' of solar energy.

curriculum.title: Course Curriculum
curriculum.description: The program combines structured theoretical knowledge with essential hands-on practical training. You'll master both the science and real-world application of solar energy systems.

theoreticalModules.title: Theoretical 1: Fundamentals of Solar PV Technology
theoreticalModules.description: Understanding PV principles, system components, panel types, and new EU Eco-design rules covering product efficiency and also durability.

theoreticalModules.title: Theoretical 2: PV System Types & Energy Storage
theoreticalModules.description: Detailing On-Grid, Off-Grid, and Hybrid systems, including battery technology and safe electrical integration.

theoreticalModules.title: Theoretical 3: Site Assessment & System Design
theoreticalModules.description: Analyzing roof angles and shading to optimize system sizing, while complying with EPBD (Solar Rooftop Initiative) mandates for new and existing buildings.

theoreticalModules.title: Theoretical 4: Project Management & Preparation
theoreticalModules.description: Covering project lifecycles, milestone tracking, and managing essential pre-installation documentation.

theoreticalModules.title: Theoretical 5: Installation Safety & Best Practices
theoreticalModules.description: Focusing on DGUV Vorschrift 3 protocols, correct PPE usage, and safe handling of DC electricity and batteries.

theoreticalModules.title: Theoretical 6: System Programming & Energy Calculation
theoreticalModules.description: Performing electric energy calculations, programming inverter settings, and testing module performance.

practicalModules.title: Practical 1: Mounting Structures & Physical Installation
practicalModules.description: Hands-on practice installing mounting structures and physically securing solar panels on both flat and sloped roofs, ensuring compliance with DIN EN 1991 and new EPBD 'solar ready' building standards.

practicalModules.title: Practical 2: Electrical Wiring & DC Connections
practicalModules.description:
Hands-on practice wiring all DC-side components, including string cabling, DC isolators, and batteries, following
 VDE 0100-712 standards.

practicalModules.title: Practical 3: Inverter Mounting & DC Commissioning
practicalModules.description: Covering the physical mounting of inverters, connecting all DC-side inputs, and preparing the AC output for final connection by a certified electrician.

practicalModules.title: Practical 4: Operations, Maintenance & Troubleshooting
practicalModules.description:
Learning systematic troubleshooting according to IEC 62446, including DC-side repair and understanding the new
 A-G Energy Labels for modules.

videoSection.title: Training in Action
videoSection.description: Watch interviews with previous participants of our program.

accreditation.title: A Certification That Opens Doors Across Europe
accreditation.description: This curriculum is being developed with support from InnoEnergy, Solar Power Europe and the BMWE to create a new, EU-wide standard for Solar Technicians. This qualification is designed to give participants energy autonomy and career mobility with European employers powering the energy transition.

finalCTA.title: Ready to Begin Your Solar Career?
finalCTA.subtitle: Apply for the free Berlin pilot program today. Bremerhaven Facility launching late 2026.
